titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Veronensis
VL4
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Jo Lc Mc

decoDesc.p: Silver ink with the first page of each gospel and nomina sacra in gold ink

listBibl.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.
Colour digital images on CD in ITSEE taken by Roberto Favalli in 2003. Images online at http://arts-itsee.bham.ac.uk/AnaServer?verbum+0+start.anv+ms=4
There is also an edition: E.S. Buchanan, The Four Gospels from the Codex Veronensis, OOLBT 6, Oxford, 1911.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 121r-215r. The manuscript is missing John 7:44-8:12. Ammonian sections and Eusebian canons are present but not recorded in the transcription. Quotations are indicated by a diple. The manuscript distinguishes between m-bars and n-bars. The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. Running titles have been transcribed where extant. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Corrections to John 4:15, 4:20 and 4:42, and change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Folio 121r, Lines 1-9: end of Matthew. Lines 10-11: Explicit of Matthew. Column 2 of Folio 121r blank.
Folio 122r: whole page in gold ink. The obelus is a vertical cross with rho.
For eum, Buchanan reads eum
For cognouit, Buchanan reads cognobit·
Buchanan reads the whole word in
For sanguine, Buchanan reads sanguinib·
For autem, Buchanan reads autem
For enarrauit, Buchanan reads enarrabit
bethania¬ or bethania÷
† in left margin in red ink s.m. Ink similar to corrections on next folio
For uidet, Buchanan reads uidit
For quoniam, Buchanan reads quonia¬
For eum, Buchanan reads eum
For iohanne, Buchanan reads iohanne¬
For eum, Buchanan reads eum
Buchanan omits the punctuation after istrahel
For habent, Buchanan reads habent fili
Buchanan uocabit
For uinum, Buchanan reads binum
For oues, Buchanan reads obes
After faciebat, Buchanan reads the punctuation ·
For farisaeis, Buchanan reads pharisaeis
Buchanan omits the punctuation after tibi
After denuo, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
After sic, Buchanan adds et
For omnis, Buchanan reads omnes
Buchanan reads the whole word uitam
For nondum, Buchanan reads non{[dum]
Buchanan reads the whole word iohannis
Buchanan reads the whole word signauit
After eius, Buchanan reads the punctuation ·
For [fi]lio, Buchanan reads [fili]o
In this verse, Buchanan reads: iacob· for [iaco]b·| [Ih¬s i]gitur for [Ih¬s] igitur | fa/[ti]gatus (with a line break) for fati/[g]atus.
Johannis cap. 4 is written in an early modern hand in the top margin above column 2 of Folio 134r
For qui, Buchanan reads [q]ui
For illi, Buchanan reads ei
For u¬de, Buchanan reads unde
For [hunc], Buchanan reads [hoc]
For [om]nis qui bib[e]rit, Buchanan reads o[m]nis qui biberit.
Buchanan omits the punctuation after hora
For Reliquit, Buchanan reads reliquid
For lasborastis, Buchanan reads laborastis
After crediderunt, Buchanan adds illi
After incipiebat, Buchanan adds eni¬
For aquam, Buchanan reads aqua
For ·xxx et uiii·, Buchanan reads ·xxx· et ·uiii·
For grabattum, Buchanan reads grauattum
Buchanan reads sanus factus es, with no correction noted
After filio, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
For transit de morte, Buchanan reads transiit de morte¬
After uoluntatem, Buchanan adds ea¬ with underdots for deletion
For eam, Buchanan reads ea
For ipsae, Buchanan reads ipse
For ualdissimo, Buchanan reads ualidissimo
At the beginning of John 6:30, Buchanan reads no space or capital letter D
For manducauerunt, Buchanan reads manducaberunt
Buchanan reads mu¬do (with no line break?)
For Dicit, Buchanan reads Dixit
For occidere, Buchanan reads occideret with the t underdotted
Buchanan reads as follows: dere et nemo / misit manu¬ / manus iniecit
For farisae, Buchanan reads farisaei
For aquae, Buchanan reads atquae, with a deletion point under the t
Buchanan omits the punctuation after nondum

Buchanan, page viii-ix, notes: "The two centre leaves of Q[uire] xiii which contained the pericope de adultera have vanished and left no trace behind. The passage has not been erased. The text of b must have been closely akin to that of ff; for the two lost leaves in b would be exactly filled by the text of ff."
For occidere, Buchanan reads hoccidere
Buchanan omits the punctuation after processi
Buchanan omits the punctuation after estis
Buchanan gives the line division as daemo/nium
Buchanan reads the whole of expuit in the last line of column 1.
Buchanan omits the punctuation after obserbat
For interrogauerunt, Buchanan reads interrogaberunt
Buchanan omits the punctuation after sumus
Buchanan omits the punctuation after ei
Buchanan has the line division dix/erunt
Buchanan omits the punctuation after exiet
After patre, Buchanan adds meo
In John 10:24, Buchanan does not note correction
Buchanan reads bethania¬: we cannot confirm the ¬
For dormit, Buchanan reads dormiit
Buchanan omits the punctuation after manifeste
After meus, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
After omnes, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
For nouum, Buchanan reads nobum
Buchanan has the line division diligo / patrem
This nomen sacrum is written in silver ink, not gold
For e[rgo ue], Buchanan reads ergo [ue]
For scan[dalize]mini, Buchanan reads scan[dali]zemini
For iusti[tia], Buchanan reads iustit[ia]
After gaudebit, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
For [u]enit, Buchanan reads uenit
Buchanan omits the punctuation after mu¬dum
For m[i]sisti, Buchanan reads misisti· (adding punctuation)
For hi, Buchanan reads hii
For e[t me], Buchanan reads sicut et tu [me]
The first two lines of this column (nazoreum ... ego) were visible to Buchanan in their entirety.
For [u]num, Buchanan reads un[u¬]
Buchanan reads the whole line, cutus sum
For perhibe, Buchanan reads [pe]rhibe
Buchanan reads pascha uultis, omitting the punctuation ·
For eu[m], Buchanan reads e{[u¬]
For di¬, Buchanan reads dii
After caesarem, Buchanan has the punctuation ·
After magdalene, Buchanan has the punctuation ·
For [mi]lites, Buchanan reads [m]ilites
For aloes, Buchanan reads aloin
For nouum, Buchanan reads nobu¬
For prio[r], Buchanan reads prior
For su[p]er, Buchanan reads super
For hortulanus, Buchanan reads ortulanus
After maria, Buchanan omits the punctuation
For [ma]gister·, Buchanan reads {[m]agister·
For insufflauit, Buchanan reads insufflabit
For cred[en]tes, Buchanan reads cre[den]tes
For [a], Buchanan reads a
Buchanan has the line division carbones / incensos et pis
For prendi[di]stis, Buchanan reads prendidistis
After est, Buchanan adds the punctuation ·
For resur / rexit, Buchanan has the line division re / surrexit
For petr[us quia], Buchanan reads petru[s quia]
